products.title: Tulip 'Jacuzzi'
products.body_html:
Tulip 'Jacuzzi' brings a cool, gently luminous beauty to the spring garden, as though someone has stirred lilac paint into moonlight. Its cup-shaped flowers open in soft pale purple, with silvery white touches brushed across the outer petals, giving each bloom a refined, almost pearlescent finish. Reaching up to around 50cm in flower, Tulip 'Jacuzzi' has just the right height for weaving through borders, lifting patio pots, or adding a quietly glamorous note to a cut flower arrangement. The broadly lance-shaped grey-green leaves provide an elegant foil, keeping the overall effect fresh, poised and never too sweet.

Plant Tulip 'Jacuzzi' where its subtle colouring can be properly admired, perhaps among creamy narcissi, dusky violas, silver foliage or deep plum tulips for a little garden theatre. It is a perennial bulb with a graceful habit, returning best in well-drained soil and a sunny spot, where the bulbs can rest undisturbed after flowering. In containers, Tulip 'Jacuzzi' looks beautifully composed, particularly in stone, terracotta or zinc, where its cool tones feel effortlessly chic. For gardeners who favour elegance over extravagance, Tulip 'Jacuzzi' is a splendid choice, sophisticated, serene and just a little bit indulgent.

products.title: Melittis 'Royal Velvet Distinction'
products.body_html:
Melittis 'Royal Velvet Distinction' is a quietly intriguing perennial, the sort of plant that rewards those who lean in for a closer look. Compact and clump-forming, this bastard balm grows to around 40cm tall and wide, making a refined choice for the front of a border, a lightly shaded path edge or a woodland-style planting. Its oval, deeply veined green leaves have scalloped edges and a softly hairy texture, matched by hairy stems with a gentle honey scent. Then, in late spring and early summer, Melittis 'Royal Velvet Distinction' lifts the scene with small whorls of orchid-like flowers, each one combining a dark purple lower lip with a clean white upper lip.

There is something rather painterly about Melittis 'Royal Velvet Distinction', as though each bloom has been given a careful brushstroke of ink and ivory. Plant it in humus-rich, well-drained soil in dappled shade or gentle sun, where its neat habit and scented foliage can be appreciated without shouting for attention. As a bastard balm, Melittis 'Royal Velvet Distinction' pairs beautifully with ferns, epimediums, hardy geraniums and spring bulbs, adding a poised note among softer leaves and naturalistic planting. Bees are likely to take an interest too, proving that good taste is not limited to gardeners. Elegant, aromatic and pleasingly uncommon, Melittis melissophyllum 'Royal Velvet Distinction' brings subtle distinction to small gardens and considered borders alike.

products.title: Lewisia 'Safira Coral'
products.body_html:
Lewisia 'Safira Coral' is a small-scale alpine with a surprisingly sunny temperament, forming a neat rosette of fleshy green leaves before bursting into colour in late spring and early summer. This charming cliff maids variety sends up branching stems carrying coral-pink flowers with a silky sheen, each bloom opening like a miniature parasol above the foliage. The shade is warm, lively and flattering, bright enough to catch the eye without barging into the garden like a brass band. Compact and well behaved, Lewisia 'Safira Coral' is particularly effective in alpine beds, gravel gardens and rockeries, where its tidy form and jewel-like flowers can be properly admired.

For best results, plant Lewisia 'Safira Coral' in very well-drained soil in a sunny, sheltered position, keeping it away from prolonged winter wet. Pots and troughs suit it beautifully, allowing drainage to be kept sharp and the flowers to be enjoyed close up on a patio or doorstep. Pair Lewisia 'Safira Coral' with thyme, saxifrages, small sedums or pale gravel for a natural alpine display with polished colour and pleasing texture. Low-growing, cheerful and quietly elegant, Lewisia cotyledon 'Safira Coral' brings a warm coral glow to smaller spaces, proving that a plant need not be tall to make a memorable entrance.

products.title: Lewisia 'Safira Tropic Pink'
products.body_html:
Lewisia 'Safira Tropic Pink' is a compact little charmer with the colour dial turned firmly towards joyful. From a low rosette of fleshy green leaves, branching stems rise in late spring and early summer, carrying pink flowers edged in white with a soft, glowing centre. The effect is fresh, sugary and sunlit, rather like a raspberry ripple served in a very elegant dish. Despite its dainty scale, Lewisia 'Safira Tropic Pink' has real presence, bringing bright detail to alpine beds, rockeries, gravel gardens and the front of sunny borders.

Give Lewisia 'Safira Tropic Pink' sharply drained soil and a sheltered, sunny position, and it will reward you with neat foliage and a cheerful flush of bloom just when the garden is gathering pace. As with many cliff maids, it is especially happy in pots, troughs or raised beds where drainage can be kept satisfyingly crisp. Try Lewisia 'Safira Tropic Pink' among stones, thyme, saxifrages or other compact alpines for a natural, mountain-garden feel. Pretty but not precious, Lewisia cotyledon 'Safira Tropic Pink' is ideal for adding polished colour to small spaces.

products.title: Lewisia 'Safira Violet'
products.body_html:
Lewisia 'Safira Violet' is one of those petite plants that earns its place by being quietly impeccable, then suddenly rather dazzling. Forming a low rosette of fleshy green leaves, this charming cliff maids variety keeps a neat profile at the front of a border, in an alpine bed or tucked into a sunny rockery crevice. In late spring and early summer, branching stems rise above the foliage carrying vivid violet-pink flowers with a bright, silky finish. The blooms have a fresh, almost enamelled quality, bringing jewel-like colour to small spaces where every detail matters. Compact but far from forgettable, Lewisia 'Safira Violet' is perfect for gardeners who like their treasures close at hand.

Give Lewisia 'Safira Violet' very well-drained soil and a sunny, sheltered position, and it will reward you with polished foliage and cheerful seasonal colour. It is especially lovely in pots, where its rosettes can be admired at eye level and kept away from winter wet, the usual nemesis of alpine plants with good taste. Try Lewisia 'Safira Violet' with gravel, stone, thyme or other compact alpines for a natural, collected look. Low-growing, bright and beautifully formed, Lewisia cotyledon 'Safira Violet' brings a little mountain-garden glamour to patios, rockeries and troughs, without asking for much more than sharp drainage and a sunny spot.

products.title: Digitalis 'Apple Blossom'
products.body_html:
Digitalis 'Apple Blossom' is a foxglove with the look of a carefully watercoloured botanical plate, all soft washes and clever details. From a rosette of green leaves rise tall, elegant spires carrying tubular, orchid-shaped flowers in a beautifully unusual blend. The lower petals are pale pink and white, freckled with deeper rose, while the upper petals glow creamy yellow, as though each bloom has caught a little spring sunshine. It feels refined rather than sugary, with just enough mischief in its colouring. Flowering in late spring and early summer, Digitalis purpurea 'Apple Blossom' brings a fresh, romantic lift to borders, particularly where pastel planting needs a little architecture.

Plant Digitalis 'Apple Blossom' in well-drained soil in sun or partial shade, and let its vertical stems weave among geraniums, nepeta, alliums and airy grasses. The soft colouring makes it easy to combine, yet never dull, and the speckled throats are irresistible to bees who clearly appreciate a well-decorated doorway. Digitalis 'Apple Blossom' is especially charming in cottage gardens, wildlife-friendly schemes and relaxed mixed borders, where it adds height without heaviness. Allow a few seedheads to stand if you enjoy that pleasing, lightly self-sown spontaneity. Graceful, distinctive and quietly theatrical, Digitalis purpurea 'Apple Blossom' offers all the charm of a classic foxglove with a more painterly, peachy-pink flourish.

products.title: Digitalis 'Lucas'
products.body_html:
Digitalis 'Lucas' is a neatly composed foxglove with a pleasingly upright habit, bringing height, colour and a touch of old-fashioned romance without overwhelming the border. Forming tidy clumps of dark green, wrinkled leaves with softly toothed edges, it has an attractive presence even before the flowers arrive. From early summer into autumn, Digitalis 'Lucas' produces elegant stems lined with tubular pink blooms, each one opening to reveal a pale, speckled throat. The effect is graceful rather than shouty, making Digitalis 'Lucas' especially useful in cottage-style schemes, wildlife gardens and mixed perennial plantings.

Plant Digitalis 'Lucas' in well-drained soil in sun or partial shade, where it will settle easily among hardy geraniums, astrantias, grasses and other relaxed border companions. Its vertical flower spikes give structure to softer planting, while the nectar-rich blooms are a welcome invitation to bees and other pollinators. As a semi-evergreen biennial, Digitalis 'Lucas' offers a generous season of interest, with flowers that feel fresh, romantic and quietly distinctive. It is a lovely choice for gardeners who enjoy classic foxgloves, but prefer their drama delivered with a little more polish and restraint.

products.title: Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink'
products.body_html:
Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink' is a foxglove with no intention of blending quietly into the border. Tall, poised and decidedly theatrical, it sends up strong spires packed with generously sized, tubular pink blooms from June to August. Each flower is softly speckled within, a detail that rewards closer inspection while the overall effect delivers colour and height from several paces away. Despite its XXL credentials, Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink' keeps all the old cottage-garden romance one expects from a foxglove, pairing beautifully with airy grasses, hardy geraniums, salvias and other midsummer perennials.

Plant Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink' in well-drained soil in sun or partial shade, where its upright stems can bring structure without demanding too much fuss. Bees and other pollinators are quick to approve, disappearing into the spotted throats with enviable enthusiasm. Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink' is particularly effective threaded through mixed plantings, used as a vertical accent near paths, or gathered in small groups for a more dramatic flourish. Elegant, colourful and just a little bit showy, Digitalis 'Lucas XXL Pink' is a splendid choice for gardeners who like their borders with height, movement and a proper sense of occasion.

products.title: Digitalis 'Berry Canary'
products.body_html:
Compact, upright and cheerfully refined, Digitalis 'Berry Canary' is a foxglove made for gardeners who like their summer colour with a little polish. From a basal cluster of dark, virtually evergreen leaves, Digitalis 'Berry Canary' sends up branched flower spikes that keep the display moving for weeks on end. The outward-facing, bell-shaped flowers are pink on the outside, then open to reveal creamy interiors speckled with pink, a charming bit of decoration that looks far more painstaking than it is. Neat enough for containers yet expressive in the border, Digitalis × valinii 'Berry Canary' brings vertical rhythm without overwhelming its neighbours, which is always good manners in a mixed planting.

Use Digitalis 'Berry Canary' to weave soft height through sunny or lightly shaded borders, where its dark foliage makes an elegant foil for pastel perennials, silver leaves and airy grasses. Its long summer flowering season gives pollinators plenty to enjoy, while the compact habit makes it especially useful near paths, patios and smaller garden spaces. Plant Digitalis 'Berry Canary' in fertile, well-drained soil and keep it watered during dry spells to encourage continuous performance. Graceful, colourful and quietly industrious, Digitalis × valinii 'Berry Canary' has the classic foxglove silhouette with a fresh, fruit-and-cream finish, looking as lovely in a cottage garden as it does in a contemporary perennial scheme.

products.title: Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue'
products.body_html:
Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' is a beautifully composed false indigo, bringing a refined play of colour and structure to the sunny border. Upright, dark stems rise with quiet confidence from neat clumps of blue-green foliage, each leaf held in those pleasing sets of three that make Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' look tidy even before the flowers appear. In late spring to early summer, deep purple buds gather like ink drops before opening into soft yellow blooms, a delicious contrast that gives this perennial its pudding-worthy name. Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' has a natural elegance without feeling precious, suiting relaxed prairie-style planting as happily as a more curated cottage garden scheme.

Once the flowers fade, Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' continues to earn its place with handsome black seed pods, adding texture and a touch of drama to the border. This is the sort of herbaceous perennial that appreciates patience, settling in to form a long-lived, drought-tolerant clump that asks for little once established. Pair Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' with ornamental grasses, salvias, echinacea or airy umbellifers for a planting that feels luminous, layered and quietly sophisticated. Loved by pollinators and wonderfully low fuss, Baptisia 'Lemon Meringue' is a sunny-border stalwart with just enough mischief in its colouring to keep things interesting.
